name: caveman-compress
tagline: Compresses CLAUDE.md/todo/preference files into terse caveman-style text via a bundled python script, backing up the original.
verdict: Clear triggers, concrete steps, defined output, and good retry/failure handling. But re-running on an already-compressed file overwrites the true backup with compressed content (no idempotency check), and its python3 + Claude-API dependency chain is never declared upfront.

highlights.summary: 'Compressed version overwrites the original file' with no check for prior compression: a second run backs up already-compressed text over the real original, then re-compresses it.
Runs 'python3 -m scripts' and has the script call the Claude API with no Requirements section or availability check for python3 or an API key.
No version field, changelog, or dated revision notes anywhere in the skill.
